title: Draw from the Ember
intro: The coven's mana runs low, and the hearth-rune burns bright and close. The Ember itself is power — you could draw on it directly. But to reach into the Sacred Flame for your own workings is to draw its Regard, and the Flame remembers who takes from it.

advisors.text: The Ember is not a well to be dipped, Archon. Draw on it and it will look back at us — and you know what its looking costs.
It is power, and we are starving for it. A keeper who will not use the fire is only a caretaker of ashes.

choices.label: Draw power straight from the hearth-rune

win.text: You reach into the Ember and it gives — a flood of raw mana into every waiting rune. It gives, and it notices.
win.saga: You drew mana straight from the Ember; it filled the coven's reserves, and the Flame's Regard turned toward you.

choices.label: Take only a careful ember's worth

win.text: You take a bare, careful measure — enough to matter, little enough that the Flame barely stirs.
win.saga: You drew a careful measure of mana from the Ember, and the Flame barely stirred at it.

win.text: You take your hand from the fire. The coven will make do — and the Flame's regard is not one hair the closer.
win.saga: You refused to draw on the Ember for mere workings, and the coven took heart from your restraint.
